title: How should Grill Visuals replace Sideshow without breaking Grill Me?
summary: The rollout proves local quality and safety first, then adds explicit public sharing, dogfoods real sessions, and only finally changes the default.

options.label: Finish local release gates
options.description: Completes family coverage, stress checks, and dogfood before changing the Grill Me default.

options.label: Ship public sharing early
options.description: Accelerates teammate access but pulls the highest-blast-radius capability ahead of local proof.

options.label: Retain Sideshow indefinitely
options.description: Avoids migration risk but keeps the weaker visual system and duplicated workflow.

events.label: Lock the contract
events.description: Choose seven bounded families and a local-first trust model

events.label: Prove architecture
events.description: Prove polished canvas behavior with safe static output

events.label: Complete all families
events.description: Ship strict schemas, native renderers, and local examples
events.detail: This removes the largest functional gap before dogfood.

events.label: Stress and accessibility
events.description: Exercise maximum inputs, long sessions, keyboard, contrast, and mobile

events.label: Add explicit sharing
events.description: Deploy and unshare only owned Cloudflare artifacts
events.detail: Public disclosure is the highest-blast-radius capability.

events.label: Dogfood two sessions
events.description: Use real migrations and bugs to find comprehension failures

events.label: Change the default
events.description: Pin a release in Grill Me and retain a narrow fallback
